task: Write a Python function range_summary(nums) taking a list of integers, possibly unsorted with duplicates, and returning a string of the distinct values in ascending order, collapsing consecutive runs into 'start-end' and joining pieces with commas, so [1, 2, 3, 5] becomes '1-3,5'. A single number stays a single number, never a range. An empty list returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function range_summary(nums) correctly summarizes a list of integers into a string of ranges, with the desired behavior for an empty list
trace: I used a combination of Python's list slicing and control structures to iterate through the input list. I kept track of the starting value and current value in order to determine when to append a range or an individual number to the `pieces` list. The edge case that I handled is when the input list is empty, in which case an empty string is returned as per the task requirements. I made the code concise by using ternary expressions for some conditional statements and eliminating unnecessary variables.
